subject: Re: Warp/M <--> Relion
body:
Hi Jesse,

Don't worry about the posts, I'm a big fan of asking questions in public.
Good luck getting this figured out!

Cheers,

Alister

On Wed, 9 Oct 2024 at 11:27, Jesse Hansen <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:

> hi Alister,
>
> you are correct, I'm doing a lot of custom workflows.  I apologize for so
> many posts.  It also helps me really get a good hang of the different
> softwares when I can dig into them like this ;). But yes, I agree this is
> absolutely user error, just trying to figure out what error I'm making...
>
> 3D subtomos from RELION and warp do indeed generate reasonable maps, which
> is reassuring.  When I try to import the Relion particles into M I can't
> get a reasonable reconstruction. These same coordinates in Desktop M give
> me a 4.5A map (but I can't process multispecies because of memory issues,
> so I'm moving to Linux M).  I'll keep chuggin away and see if I can figure
> this out...
>
> Extract command is below.
>
> WarpTools ts_export_particles \
>
> --settings warp_tiltseries.settings \
>
> --input_directory warp_tiltseries/coordinates \
>
> --input_pattern "*.tomostar.star" \
>
> --coords_angpix 11.056 \
>
> --output_star relion/allparticles_bin8_3D.star \
>
> --output_angpix 11.056 \
>
> --box 40 \
>
> --diameter 156 \
>
> --relative_output_paths \
>
> --3d
> thanks again,
> J
> On Wednesday, October 9, 2024 at 4:43:26 PM UTC+2 alist...@gmail.com
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Jesse,
>>
>> Based on your recent messages you’ve been doing lots of ‘custom
>> workflows’ - given that Warp/M appears to be working well under normal
>> circumstances I’d suggest there is likely some user error 🤷‍♂️
>>
>> What does your particle export command look like?
>>
>> You could generate 3D subtomos from the same particles in both packages
>> and check that they reconstruct correctly with relion_reconstruct as a
>> sanity check. This would exclude the variability of the 3D refinement…
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>>
>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>
>> On Oct 9, 2024, at 01:19, Jesse Hansen <bluu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿WarpTools/2.0.0dev28 and Relion 5.0 beta3 commit 12cf15.
>>
>>
>> I imported tilt series alignments into Relion and Warp. I generate
>> tomograms and they are perfectly aligned with one another.  However, I
>> cannot successfully move particles between RELION and Warp/M.
>>
>> 1) Particles extracted in Relion refines well to 7A.  I import into M,
>> run first round of refinement (iter 0) and it produces a blob at 50A.
>>
>> 2) Same particles as above are instead extracted in Warp. Run a
>> refinement in RELION and it gets stuck at 30A and overall is poor quality
>> map.
>>
>> any thoughts are welcome.
>>
>> J
>>
>> --
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups
>> "Warp" group.
>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an
>> email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
>> To view this discussion on the web visit
>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/a9bea847-b79c-4af4-a092-645280a3696cn%40googlegroups.com
>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/a9bea847-b79c-4af4-a092-645280a3696cn%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>> .
>>
